engineering.translation: The disciplined one (yuktaḥ), having abandoned the fruit of action (karma-phalam tyaktvā), attains the peace founded on naiṣṭhikīm (the established/abiding state). The undisciplined (ayuktaḥ), driven by desire (kāma-kāreṇa), attached to the fruit, is bound. Krishna draws the contrast sharply: the same action, performed with or without attachment to fruit, produces opposite results. The disciplined attain peace; the undisciplined are bound. The verse coheres directly with 2.47-2.48 and is its operational restatement at chapter 5's juncture. The engineering analog: the engineer who ships the migration without grasping at the launch-metric attains operational peace; the engineer who ships the same migration while obsessing over the launch-metric is bound by the attachment. Same merge, same code; different inner state, different outcome at the level the verse cares about.
engineering.concrete_scenario: Two senior engineers ship the same kind of migration on the same Friday. Engineer A has prepared the runbook on GitHub, run the staging Datadog tests, scheduled the Slack announcement, and sized the rollback window. She ships, watches the dashboards, files the post-deploy notes, closes the PagerDuty pre-emptive alert, and at 6 PM she is at peace — the work was offered to the team's sustaining loop, the metric will be what it will be. Engineer B has shipped a structurally identical migration with the same level of preparation. But across Saturday morning he refreshes Datadog every twenty minutes, reads Slack obsessively for executive reactions, drafts and redrafts a launch tweet, calculates the visible impact on his promotion case. The same code is in production; engineer A is at peace, engineer B is bound. The action was identical; the discipline was different; the verse names the difference structurally.
engineering.falsifiability: The analog fails if a reader concludes engineer A doesn't read dashboards. She reads them — but she reads them as feedback, not as the source of her state. Detachment from outcome-as-identity is not detachment from outcome-as-data.
engineering.counter_example: When the migration genuinely has gone wrong and requires sustained attention — when Datadog refreshes are operationally needed because the cascade is unfolding — engineer B's behaviour is correct. The verse names the structural condition (attachment-to-fruit produces bondage), not a flat prescription about attention to dashboards.
engineering.implication: On your next Friday-shipped migration, observe Saturday morning. The peace is the readable signature.
engineering.quotable_line: Same merge, same code, same Friday. The disciplined are at peace by Saturday; the undisciplined are bound.

iterations.mutation: v0 generation: BG 5.12 engineering layer drafted from triangulated source pack. Anchored to chapter-5 thesis: equivalence-of-paths-with-karma-yoga-preference, decoupled identity, sama-darśana, internal-bliss-source, brahma-nirvāṇa-as-operational-rung-with-honest-STRETCHED. Cross-references to chapters 2-4 preserved.
